description: Evaluates how enterprise security governance is established, operated, measured, and enforced across the organization (decision rights, oversight, policy/standards lifecycle, risk acceptance and exceptions, reporting, assurance, and accountability).

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: The enterprise security governance function is established, funded, and operating effectively with clear scope and outcomes.
evaluation_focus: Documented governance charter and scope
Operating model, stakeholders, and delivery cadence
Continuous improvement and stakeholder feedback

capabilities.name: Governance Metrics
capabilities.description: Governance defines measurable outcomes and uses KPIs/KRIs and reporting to drive decisions, prioritize investments, and communicate risk to leadership.
evaluation_focus: Defined KPIs/KRIs tied to governance outcomes
Leadership reporting cadence and audience (exec/board)
Evidence that metrics inform decisions and prioritization

capabilities.name: Governance Responsibility Model
capabilities.description: Roles, committees, and decision rights are defined and operating for security governance, including escalation paths and accountability mechanisms.
evaluation_focus: Defined governance bodies, responsibilities, and decision scope
Documented RACI for key security decisions
Clear escalation paths for high-risk decisions and exceptions

capabilities.name: Governance leader
capabilities.description: A governance leader (or accountable owner) exists with authority, sponsorship, and responsibility for security governance outcomes across the enterprise.
evaluation_focus: Named leader with clear mandate and delegated authority
Engagement model with executives and delivery stakeholders
Accountability for governance decisions and outcomes

capabilities.name: User Segmentation Strategy
capabilities.description: A user segmentation strategy exists to differentiate identities by risk, privilege, and exposure, with governance for segmentation criteria and lifecycle management.
evaluation_focus: Defined segmentation criteria (roles, privilege tiers, risk levels)
Governed lifecycle for user categories and access models
Alignment to policy requirements and identity governance processes

capabilities.name: Workload Segmentation Strategy (Micro-Segmentation)
capabilities.description: A micro-segmentation strategy exists for workloads and applications, including policies, enforcement mechanisms, exceptions, and measurement.
evaluation_focus: Defined micro-segmentation principles and target scope
Enforcement design (policy-as-code, agents, service mesh, etc.)
Exception governance and measurement of effectiveness

capabilities.name: Network Segmentation Strategy (Macro-segmentation)
capabilities.description: A macro-segmentation strategy exists for networks and environments (on-prem and cloud), including zoning, controls, exceptions, and monitoring.
evaluation_focus: Defined segmentation zones and allowable traffic patterns
Control enforcement (firewalls, NAC, cloud segmentation, etc.)
Exception governance and monitoring of segmentation health

capabilities.name: External Expert Reference
capabilities.description: Governance incorporates external expert input or benchmarks (advisory, threat intelligence, peer benchmarks, audit/assurance) to improve decisions and validate direction.
evaluation_focus: Use of independent expertise or benchmarks to inform governance
Evidence of periodic external review or validation
Clear mechanism to incorporate findings into decisions and improvements

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
